name: St. Claude Avenue Bridge — Lower Piling Access
description: The concrete piling at the base of the bridge's western approach, accessible by a rusted service ladder, where Darnell Arceneaux's flat-bottom aluminum skiff knocks against the concrete in the brown current.
atmosphericDetails: The pharmaceutical crate stenciled with a red cross sits in the skiff's bow, its cardboard corners dark with water absorption
Three children sit in the stern of the skiff, their feet not touching the flood water, watching the ladder
The motor at the skiff's transom ticks as it cools — it has been running for four hours straight
